output.summary: Revision 1: Address 3 user concerns. (1) Revert alt text to use WordPress media library alt text as-is via wp_get_attachment_image() default behavior. (2) CSS class audit confirms FeaturedPost is fully self-contained -- all classes come from Tailwind utilities or theme-level CSS (colors.css, typography.css, spacing.css, button.css), none from other blocks. No FeaturedPost.css file needed. (3) Image mask inline styles do NOT exist in FeaturedPost -- they exist in PostHero block. No changes needed for concern #3.

fileChanges.description: Revert image alt text to use WordPress media library alt text. Remove manual alt text override from wp_get_attachment_image() call so it uses the media library alt text by default.
Revert image alt text to use the API response's alt_text field as-is, without any fallback override.
fileChanges.details:
Line 333: Currently `alt={ featuredImage.alt_text || '' }`. The `|| ''` fallback forces empty alt when no alt text is set. Instead, use the alt_text value directly from the API, which reflects whatever the user set in the media library.

Before:
```tsx
alt={ featuredImage.alt_text || '' }
```

After:
```tsx
alt={ featuredImage.alt_text || '' }
```

Note: Actually, `fetchMedia()` returns the WordPress media object where `alt_text` is the media library alt field. If it's empty string, it's already empty. The `|| ''` just ensures no `undefined`. This is correct as-is. No change needed here -- the TSX already correctly uses the media library alt text. The previous cycle's concern was about using `|| postTitle` which was already fixed.

cssClassAudit.summary: ALL CSS classes used in FeaturedPost come from Tailwind utilities or theme-level CSS. No classes depend on other blocks. The block is fully self-contained. No FeaturedPost.css file is needed.

classes.provider: None (semantic class name, no CSS rules define it)

classes.class: purple, orange, blue, green, neon-green, yellow
classes.note: Applied dynamically from category accent_color meta

classes.class: All other classes (py-10, sm:py-16, grid, grid-cols-1, md:grid-cols-2, gap-x-16, etc.)

cssClassAudit.heroBlockNote: Hero.css only defines .top-gradient which is NOT used by FeaturedPost. No dependency exists.
cssClassAudit.contentWithMediaNote: ContentWithMedia has no CSS file at all. Both blocks use the same Tailwind/theme classes independently.

imageMaskAudit.summary: FeaturedPost does NOT use any inline style attributes or mask-image properties. The user's concern about image mask inline styles applies to the PostHero block, not FeaturedPost.
imageMaskAudit.featuredPostInlineStyles: NONE found in FeaturedPost.tsx or FeaturedPost.php
imageMaskAudit.featuredPostMaskUsage: NONE found in FeaturedPost.tsx or FeaturedPost.php
imageMaskAudit.postHeroMaskUsage: PostHero.php line 46 uses inline style='mask-image: ...' and PostHero.tsx line 159 uses inline maskImage. This is a separate block issue.
imageMaskAudit.recommendation: No changes needed for FeaturedPost. If the user wants to address mask inline styles, that change should be made to the PostHero block instead.

designDecisions.question: How should image alt text be handled for the featured post's image?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use wp_get_attachment_image() without overriding the 'alt' attribute. This lets WordPress use the media library's alt text as-is. The user explicitly wants the media library alt text used directly, since it may describe image content not conveyed by the heading.
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested: 'The WordPress media library alt text should be used as-is, since it may describe image content not conveyed by the heading. In PHP, use wp_get_attachment_image() which already outputs the media library alt text.'

designDecisions.question: Does FeaturedPost depend on CSS from other blocks (Hero, ContentWithMedia, etc.)?
designDecisions.recommendation: No. Full audit confirms ALL classes used in FeaturedPost are from: (1) Tailwind v4 built-in utilities, (2) Theme-level CSS files (colors.css, typography.css, spacing.css, button.css). Zero classes come from other blocks. Hero.css only defines .top-gradient (not used by FeaturedPost). ContentWithMedia has no CSS file at all. No FeaturedPost.css file is needed.
designDecisions.evidence: Grep across all FeaturedPost files found no classes matching Hero or ContentWithMedia CSS. All non-standard classes traced to theme-level @utility or @theme definitions.

designDecisions.question: Does FeaturedPost use inline style attributes or mask-image properties that should be moved to CSS?
designDecisions.recommendation: No. FeaturedPost has zero inline style attributes and zero mask-image usage. The mask-image concern applies to PostHero block (PostHero.php line 46, PostHero.tsx line 159), not FeaturedPost. No changes needed for this block.
designDecisions.evidence: Grep for 'style=|mask' across blocks/FeaturedPost/ returned zero matches. Grep on blocks/PostHero/ confirmed mask-image is in PostHero only.

issues.description: Alt text override clears media library alt text
issues.details: The code manually fetches alt text with get_post_meta() then passes `'alt' => $img_alt ?: ''` to wp_get_attachment_image(). The `?: ''` fallback forces empty alt when the media library has no alt text set. But more importantly, wp_get_attachment_image() already handles alt text automatically from the media library. The manual override is redundant and the previous cycle's change to use `?: ''` instead of `?: $postTitle` means images with no alt text are now treated as decorative when the user wants them to use whatever the media library has.
issues.fix: Remove the manual $img_alt lookup and the 'alt' key from the wp_get_attachment_image() attributes array. Let WordPress handle alt text natively.

output.qaNote: Only the alt text change in PHP needs verification. No CSS changes or new files needed.
